items.headline: Stocks Decline Amid Surging Oil Prices and Shifting Fed Expectations
items.summary: U.S. equities concluded Friday, March 21, with broad declines, marking the fourth consecutive losing week for the S&P 500. A sharp rise in crude oil prices, fueled by ongoing geopolitical tensions, has significantly altered market sentiment, pushing Treasury yields higher and causing traders to re-evaluate the likelihood of Federal Reserve interest rate cuts in 2026. Concerns over persistent inflation and potential Fed rate hikes now dominate the outlook. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Down 1.5% on Friday, marking fourth straight losing week.

items.headline: Tilly's, Inc. Surges in March
items.summary: Tilly's, Inc. ($TLYS) recorded a significant gain of 167.4% through March 21, leading the monthly performance for stocks with market caps over $100 million.

items.headline: Sable Offshore Corp. Sees Strong Monthly Performance
items.summary: Sable Offshore Corp. ($SOC) was among the top monthly performers, climbing 89.3% by March 21.

items.headline: Babcock & Wilcox Enterprise Climbs
items.summary: Babcock & Wilcox Enterprise ($BW) saw a substantial monthly gain of 68.67% by March 21.

items.headline: Tencent Music Entertainment Group Leads Weekly Losers
items.summary: Tencent Music Entertainment Group ($TME) was the worst-performing U.S.-listed stock for the week ending March 20, falling 28.94%.

items.headline: Klarna Group Experiences Steep Weekly Decline
items.summary: Klarna Group ($KLAR) saw a 20.79% decline for the week ending March 20, trading at a significant discount to its fair value estimate.

items.headline: Regaal Resourcess Among Small-Cap Decliners
items.summary: Small-cap Regaal Resourcess experienced a 23% fall over five trading sessions ending March 20.

items.headline: Fed Rate Hike Bets Rise as Oil Prices Surge, Threatening Stagflation
items.summary: Wall Street has shifted from anticipating Federal Reserve interest rate cuts to now considering potential hikes later in 2026. This comes as surging oil prices push Brent crude above $109 and U.S. crude to $98, fueling inflation concerns and raising the risk of stagflation. The Fed held rates steady on March 18, but market-based pricing now indicates over 60% odds of a hike by October. Fed Chair Jerome Powell emphasized the central bank's independence in setting monetary policy.

items.headline: Defensive and Energy Sectors Lead Amid Risk-Off Sentiment
items.summary: A notable sector rotation is underway, with defensive sectors like Utilities ($XLU) and Consumer Staples ($XLP), along with Energy ($XLE), demonstrating leadership as risk-off sentiment prevails. Conversely, Consumer Discretionary ($XLY), Real Estate ($XLRE), and Technology ($XLK) are lagging. This shift suggests investors are prioritizing stability over growth due to economic headwinds and inflationary pressures.

items.headline: Gas Prices Climb Nationwide as Crude Oil Costs Soar
items.summary: Retail fuel prices are rising across the U.S., with the national average for regular gasoline hitting $3.925 on March 21, a 28-cent increase from the previous week. Crude oil prices remain elevated, with Brent futures trading around $112 a barrel on Friday, up 70% since the start of the Iran conflict. Economists warn that sustained high oil prices could push inflation higher and potentially trigger a recession if they reach $140-$175 a barrel.

items.headline: Oracle Reports Massive Backlog Amidst Software Rout
items.summary: Oracle ($ORCL) announced its fiscal third-quarter earnings, revealing a substantial $553 billion in remaining purchase obligations, a 325% year-over-year increase. Despite this, the stock is down approximately 21% year-to-date, caught in a broader 'software stock rout' of 2026 driven by investor fears of AI disruption, even as Oracle pivots towards an AI-heavy business model.
